title: centaurea nigra subsp. rivularis
intro: Centaurea nigra subsp. rivularis, also known as rivularis knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This perennial plant is native to Europe and is characterized by its blue to purple flowers and biennial or short-lived perennial growth habit. Centaurea nigra subsp. rivularis has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone and polyphenolic compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, and has been prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal benefits.
